seoTitle: How to Add a Hyperlink in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to insert a clickable hyperlink into a Jira issue description or comment using the built-in text editor.
intro: Linking out to documentation, related tickets, or external resources makes a Jira issue far more useful to whoever reads it later. This guide covers how to add a clickable hyperlink to an issue description or comment.

steps.heading: Open the issue field you're editing
steps.body: Click into the description or comment field on the Jira issue where you want to add the link.

steps.heading: Select the text you want to link
steps.body: Highlight the word or phrase that should become the clickable link.

steps.heading: Use the link tool in the editor
steps.body: Click the link icon in Jira's text editor toolbar, which opens a field for entering the URL.

steps.heading: Paste the URL
steps.body: Enter or paste the destination URL and confirm to apply the link to your selected text.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Save the description or submit the comment, then click the link to confirm it opens correctly.

faq.question: Can I just paste a raw URL and have it become clickable?
faq.answer: In many cases Jira will auto-detect a pasted URL and turn it into a link automatically, but using the link tool gives you control over the display text.

faq.question: Can I link to another Jira issue?
faq.answer: Yes, you can hyperlink text to another issue's URL, or use Jira's issue linking feature for a more structured connection between tickets.

faq.question: Does the hyperlink work the same way in comments and descriptions?
faq.answer: Yes, the same text editor and link tool are used in both fields, so the process is consistent.

faq.question: Why isn't my link showing as clickable after saving?
faq.answer: Double-check that the URL was entered correctly and includes the proper protocol, such as https://, since a malformed URL may not render as a link.
